data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0003/sessions/0003-horizon-T2

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next
- **Human:** fix `I-0009`, then relaunch `horizon run AJC.fbc`. The T2 leaves are self-contained and fully documented in-file.
- No further Ground action possible until a Horizon session produces a diff.

# Recommended focus
Task: T2
Title: Prove the FBC lemma — flat base change (Stacks 02KH)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange*,AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean

Close the **flat base change** route (Stacks **02KH**, Čech separated case) — the current PRIMARY directive in the project's `TO_USER.md`.

Prove, for flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$

Remaining open leaves:
- `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks **02KG**, termwise affine base change) via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` in `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange`.
- `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact).

to finish `IsIso bareBC` in `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`. The affine-base separated case suffices for **Kleiman 4.8 Step 1**.

**Done when:** `lake build` green, no new axioms; roadmap item `AJC.fbc` advances/closes.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

24	# Unconditional `Rⁱ f_*` via Čech + flat base change (target-local roadmap)
25

27	enrich merge of the `Cech-Cohomology` subproject (2026-06-18). They originally
28	lived at the tail of the target's `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean`, which
29	was replaced wholesale by the source library's (more fundamental and more
30	complete) `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`. The source development does not package

32	`CechComplex` so that the target's blueprint scope (`def:cech_higher_direct_image`,
33	`lem:cech_flat_base_change`) is preserved and its `\uses{}` graph stays intact.

35	* `cechHigherDirectImage` is sorry-free (a one-liner on the merged `CechComplex`).
36	* `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH) is now sorry-free *modulo* the following named leaves
37	  (currently 7 open `sorry`s, all blueprinted): the homology-side flat left-exactness
38	  `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (the abstract-left-adjoint wall); the two degreewise
39	  Beck–Chevalley leaves `cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` (S-level square along `g`,
40	  `naturality` residual) and `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` (X-level square along `g'`, `naturality`
41	  residual); the per-σ single-open S-level base change `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`

50	  pullback-section formula for `pullback g'` (a Mathlib gap, shared with `canonicalBaseChangeMap`;
51	  affine-`U_σ` bypass via `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine`). The abstract→affine

73	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
74	
75	/-- **Unconditional higher direct image via Čech.** For a separated quasi-compact
76	`f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`, and a quasi-coherent
77	`F : X.Modules`, the `i`-th higher direct image is the `i`-th cohomology of the
78	relative Čech complex. This needs **no** enough-injectives hypothesis on
79	`O_X`-modules: it is the cohomology of an explicit complex of quasi-coherent
80	sheaves. By `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` it agrees with the derived-functor
81	higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and is independent of the
82	chosen affine cover up to canonical isomorphism. For `i = 0` one recovers the
83	ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`. -/
84	noncomputable def cechHigherDirectImage (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover)
85	    (F : X.Modules) (i : ℕ) : S.Modules :=
86	  (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i
87	
88	/-! ### Skeleton for `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH, separated case)
89	
90	The proof decomposes into the following pieces, assembled in `cech_flatBaseChange`:
91	
92	1. **Homology side — DONE (modulo flat left-exactness).** The pullback `g^*` is exact,
93	   so it commutes with `HomologicalComplex.homology`:
94	   * `pullback_preservesFiniteColimits` — free, `g^*` is a left adjoint;
95	   * `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` — **the one genuine homology-side gap**: `g`
96	     flat ⇒ `g^*` is left-exact (Mathlib has this affine-locally for `extendScalars`,
97	     `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`, but not yet lifted
98	     through sheafification to `SheafOfModules.pullback`);
99	   * `pullback_preservesHomology` — then *derived* (no `sorry`) via
100	     `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`;
101	   * `mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso` / `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` — the
102	     complex-level upgrade of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`, **sorry-free**.
103	2. **`cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (load-bearing, Stacks 02KG) — STILL OPEN.** Applying
104	   `g^*` degreewise to `Č•(𝒰, F)` recovers `Č•(𝒰', g'^* F)`. It is supplied (iter-315) by
105	   `cechComplex_baseChange_cosimplicialIso`, the whiskered composite of the cosimplicial
106	   Beck–Chevalley iso `cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` (degreewise → per-σ
107	   `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`, the affine-reduction heart routing through the
108	   altitude-2 bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` to the **sorry-free** affine
109	   termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` via the carved
110	   `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` ring-pushout square) with the twisted-nerve
111	   identification `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` (per-σ `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`, the
112	   X-level open-immersion Beck–Chevalley `openImmersion_beckChevalley` over the
113	   cover-base-change identity `coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq`). The route uses the
114	   concrete-tilde non-mate brick, NOT the adjoint-mate machinery. This is the genuine open
115	   content of Stacks 02KG/02KH.
116	3. Assembly `cech_flatBaseChange` — **sorry-free**, reduces to 1 + 2.
117	
118	No spectral sequence is needed here: this is the *separated* case (`[IsSeparated f]`).
119	The Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence enters only in the separated → general
120	quasi-separated promotion of Stacks 02KH, which is **not** this lemma. -/
121	
122	section HomologyComm
123	
124	variable {C D : Type*} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] [Preadditive C] [Preadditive D]
125	  [CategoryWithHomology C] [CategoryWithHomology D]
126	
127	/-- **Complex-level upgrade of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`.** An additive functor `F`
128	that preserves homology commutes with `HomologicalComplex.homology`. The degree-`i`
129	short complex of `(F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K` is *definitionally* `F` applied to
130	the degree-`i` short complex `K.sc i` of `K` (both have `Xⱼ = F.obj (K.Xⱼ)` and
131	`d = F.map (K.d)`), so this is exactly `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso (K.sc i) F`. -/
132	noncomputable def mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
133	    [F.PreservesHomology] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (K : HomologicalComplex C c) (i : ι) :
134	    ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i) :=
135	  ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso (K.sc i) F
136	
137	end HomologyComm
138	
139	/-- **Flat base change has left-adjoint pullback**, hence `g^*` preserves finite
140	colimits (free: `g^* = pullback g` is a left adjoint). -/
141	instance pullback_preservesFiniteColimits (g : S' ⟶ S) :
142	    Limits.PreservesFiniteColimits (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) := inferInstance
143	
144	/-- **Flat ⇒ `g^*` is left-exact** *(STUB — the one genuine homology-side gap)*.
145	
146	The mathematical reduction is verified and worth recording. By
147	`SheafOfModules.pullbackIso`, the pullback factors as
148	`g^* ≅ forget ⋙ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification)`.
149	Two of the three factors preserve finite limits *in Mathlib already*:
150	* `SheafOfModules.forget` — `SheafOfModules.forgetPreservesFiniteLimits` (it is a right
151	  adjoint to sheafification);
152	* `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` — the instance in
153	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean` (sheafification is a
154	  left-exact reflector; needs `HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat`, which holds for the scheme
155	  site since `X.Modules` is abelian).
156	
157	So the *only* irreducible content is that the **presheaf-level** pullback
158	`PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` preserves finite limits when `g` is flat. Mathlib
159	defines this presheaf pullback purely as `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` with **no
160	pointwise description**; mathematically it is the inverse image `g⁻¹` (exact) followed
161	by extension of scalars along the flat ring map (left-exact, cf.
162	`ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`), but neither this factorisation
163	nor its left-exactness is packaged. Closing it is a genuine multi-hundred-LOC Mathlib
164	development (assembling it via `pullbackIso` additionally requires resolving the
165	`sheafification`/`HasSheafify` instances for the concrete scheme site). -/
166	/- USER (Stacks 02KH leaf 1/2): close via the reduction proved out in the docstring —
167	   transfer along `SheafOfModules.pullbackIso` and discharge `forget` + `sheafification`
168	   (both already preserve finite limits in Mathlib: `SheafOfModules.forgetPreservesFiniteLimits`,
169	   the `sheafification` instance in `Presheaf/Sheafification.lean`). The irreducible core is
170	   that `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is left-exact under flat (mathematically `g⁻¹` exact,
171	   then flat `extendScalars` left-exact via `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`).
172	   Likely path: stalkwise (stalk of pullback = `extendScalars` of stalk + pointwise flat
173	   exactness). This is pure exactness of flat pullback — no Čech/cohomology or spectral
174	   sequence is involved here (those belong to the base-change *assembly*, not this leaf).
175	   Reference: Stacks 02KH (the flatness input). -/
176	instance pullback_preservesFiniteLimits (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g] :
177	    Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) := sorry
178	
179	/-- **Flat ⇒ `g^*` preserves homology** — *derived* from left-exactness +
180	left-adjointness via `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`. No `sorry` of its own. -/
181	instance pullback_preservesHomology (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g] :
182	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).PreservesHomology := inferInstance
183	
184	/-- **`g^*` commutes with Čech homology** (flat exactness, complex level). **Sorry-free:**
185	a direct specialisation of `mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso` to `g^* = pullback g`,
186	which is additive and (for `g` flat) preserves homology. -/
187	noncomputable def pullback_mapHC_homologyIso (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g]
188	    (K : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ) (i : ℕ) :
189	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homology i
190	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (K.homology i) :=
191	  mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) K i
192	
193	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — additive functors and the alternating coface complex
194	
195	The relative Čech complex `CechComplex` is, by construction
196	(`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`), the alternating coface-map cochain complex of a
197	cosimplicial object. The leaf-2 base change `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` must move the
198	degreewise pullback `g^*` *inside* this `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` construction. The
199	following two general declarations package exactly that move at the right (cosimplicial)
200	altitude, with no reference to schemes: an additive functor `F` commutes with the
201	alternating coface map complex, naturally in the cosimplicial variable. This is
202	Mathlib-absent and is the cosimplicial-natural-iso brick flagged in
203	`analogies/02kh-leaves-304.md` (step (b)). -/
204	
205	section AlternatingCoface
206	
207	open AlgebraicTopology
208	
209	variable {C D : Type*} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] [Preadditive C] [Preadditive D]
210	
211	/-- The degree-`n` differential of the alternating coface complex is the alternating sum
212	`objD`. Project-local unfolding lemma. -/
213	private theorem alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d
214	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (n : ℕ) :
215	    ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y).d n (n + 1)
216	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y n := by
217	  simp only [alternatingCofaceMapComplex, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj, CochainComplex.of_d]
218	
219	/-- **An additive functor commutes with the alternating coface differential.** For an
220	additive functor `F : C ⥤ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y`, applying `F` to the
221	degree-`n` alternating coface differential `objD Y n = ∑ᵢ (-1)ⁱ • Yδᵢ` equals the
222	alternating coface differential of the post-composed cosimplicial object `Y ⋙ F`. This is
223	`F.map_sum` together with `Functor.map_zsmul` (both available since `F` is additive).
224	Project-local. -/
225	theorem map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
226	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (i : ℕ) :
227	    F.map (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y i)
228	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
229	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y) i := by
230	  rw [AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Functor.map_sum]
231	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
232	  intro k _
233	  rw [Functor.map_zsmul]
234	  rfl
235	
236	/-- **Additive functors commute with `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.** For an additive
237	functor `F : C ⥤ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y` in `C`, applying `F` degreewise to the
238	alternating coface map cochain complex of `Y` yields the alternating coface map cochain
239	complex of the post-composed cosimplicial object `F ∘ Y`:
240	`F.mapHomologicalComplex (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Y) ≅ alternatingCofaceMapComplex (F ∘ Y)`.
241	The degreewise components are identities (the degree-`n` terms are `F.obj (Y.obj [n])` on
242	both sides) and the differential compatibility is `map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD`.
243	This is the cosimplicial-altitude brick (step (b)) used to push `g^*` into the relative
244	Čech complex `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. Project-local Mathlib supplement. -/
245	-- (v4.31.0: `CechToHigherDirectImage` also defines a public `mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso`
246	-- — that file never compiled before the migration so the name clash was latent; now that it
247	-- builds, both being public collides at the root import. This copy is used only inside this file,
248	-- so mark it `private` to resolve the clash without rebuilding the 4.3 h `CechToHigherDirectImage`.)
249	private noncomputable def mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
250	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) :
251	    (F.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y)
252	      ≅ (alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj
253	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y) :=
254	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
255	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
256	    have h : F.map (((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y).d i (i + 1))
257	        = ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj
258	            (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y)).d i (i + 1) := by
259	      rw [alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d, alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d]
260	      exact map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD F Y i
261	    rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, h]
262	    erw [Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id])
263	
264	end AlternatingCoface
265	
266	/-- **The degreewise pullback of the relative Čech complex, in alternating-coface form.**
267	Specialising `mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso` to `F = g^* = Scheme.Modules.pullback g`
268	and the push-forward cosimplicial object underlying `CechComplex f 𝒰 F`, this identifies
269	`g^*` applied degreewise to `Č•(𝒰, F)` with the alternating coface complex of the
270	cosimplicial object obtained by post-composing the dropped Čech nerve with `f_*` then `g^*`.
271	This is the first concrete step of the leaf-2 assembly `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`:
272	it moves the degreewise pullback inside the `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` construction,
273	reducing the remaining content to a (Beck–Chevalley) natural isomorphism of the underlying
274	cosimplicial objects `(g^* ∘ f_* ∘ nerve) ≅ (f'_* ∘ g'^* ∘ nerve')`. Project-local. -/
275	noncomputable def pullback_cechComplex_alternatingIso (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S)
276	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
277	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
278	        (CechComplex f 𝒰 F)
279	      ≅ (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S'.Modules).obj
280	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering S.Modules S'.Modules).obj
281	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback g)).obj
282	            (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
